problems_encountered.problem: Direct PYTHONPATH overlay of current FlashInfer source on installed serving containers failed because nvidia-cutlass-dsl 4.4.2 lacks cutlass.cute.nvgpu.OperandMajorMode.
problems_encountered.resolution_for_validation: Upgrade nvidia-cutlass-dsl[cu13] to >=4.5.0 inside the ephemeral container.

problems_encountered.problem: Source Python over older installed FlashInfer binary/JIT modules failed in NVFP4 quantization with TVM FFI argument mismatch: expected 9 arguments but got 10.
problems_encountered.resolution_for_validation: Use an editable source install and remove stale flashinfer-jit-cache/flashinfer-cubin packages in the ephemeral layer.

problems_encountered.problem: Editable install from copied Git checkout initially failed with Git 'dubious ownership' for /tmp/flashinfer.
problems_encountered.resolution_for_validation: Add /tmp/flashinfer as a safe.directory inside the ephemeral container.

problems_encountered.problem: Editable FlashInfer 0.6.13 import rejected installed flashinfer-cubin 0.6.10 without FLASHINFER_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK or package removal.
problems_encountered.resolution_for_validation: Remove flashinfer-cubin and flashinfer-jit-cache so source JIT is used.

interpretation: Installed vLLM and SGLang containers currently exclude b12x from SM121 NVFP4 mm_fp4 auto-dispatch.
The patched FlashInfer source changes real GB10 auto-dispatch to prefer b12x.
A tiny forced-b12x NVFP4 GEMM can run on GB10 when the source package and generated JIT modules are kept version-consistent.
This is source/JIT proof, not an end-to-end vLLM or SGLang serving benchmark.
The next before/after step needs a clean container or wheel set with matching flashinfer-python, flashinfer-jit-cache, flashinfer-cubin, nvidia-cutlass-dsl, and CUDA targets.
